courses.title: Gramsci for Aotearoa
courses.description: Power, hegemony, institutions, and counter-power from below. Six lessons.

lessons.title: Course launch
lessons.teaser: Course overview and how to follow along.

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Who was Gramsci, and why read him now?
lessons.teaser: Introduction to Gramsci's life, ideas, and relevance today.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Hegemony — how ruling ideas become ordinary life
lessons.teaser: Why ruling ideas become ordinary life.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Organic Intellectuals — who speaks for the working class?
lessons.teaser: Who speaks for the working class and how?

lessons.title: Lesson 4: Civil Society, the State, and who controls the institutions
lessons.teaser: How institutions reproduce power — and how to fight back.

lessons.title: Lesson 5: War of Position — building counter-power from below
lessons.teaser: The long game of building socialist counter-power.

lessons.title: Lesson 6: Putting it all together — a socialist toolkit for Aotearoa
lessons.teaser: Applying Gramsci's framework to Aotearoa New Zealand today.

courses.title: Kropotkin & Mutual Aid
courses.description: Cooperation, survival, working-class care, and socialist practice. Six lessons, free to all.

lessons.title: Course intro — free email course from The Kiwi Dialect
lessons.teaser: Overview and how to enrol.

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Who was Kropotkin, and what is mutual aid?
lessons.teaser: An introduction to cooperation beyond capitalist myth-making.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Mutual Aid vs Social Darwinism — nature is not a war of all against all
lessons.teaser: The idea that humans are naturally selfish is ideology, not science.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Mutual Aid in Working-Class History — strikes, friendly societies, and solidarity networks
lessons.teaser: Working-class people have always built mutual aid networks to survive capitalism.

lessons.title: Lesson 4: Mutual Aid in Aotearoa — hapū, whānau, working-class communities
lessons.teaser: Mutual aid is not a Western import to Aotearoa. It was already here.

lessons.title: Lesson 5: The State vs Mutual Aid — when welfare replaced self-organised solidarity
lessons.teaser: The welfare state was a genuine gain — and also used to dismantle self-organisation.

lessons.title: Lesson 6: Mutual Aid Now — from food banks to tenants unions to climate response
lessons.teaser: Mutual aid is not theoretical. It is happening right now.

courses.title: Graeber: Debt, Bullshit Jobs & Direct Democracy
courses.description: Six lessons on debt as a tool of power, why most work is meaningless by design, and how ordinary people build direct democracy. Free, delivered by email.

lessons.title: Course intro — free email course from The Kiwi Dialect
lessons.teaser: Six lessons on Graeber's big ideas — free. Enrol here.

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Who was Graeber, and why does anthropology make you dangerous?
lessons.teaser: Anthropology, anarchism, and seeing the world differently.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Debt — the first 5,000 years and why you should never feel morally obligated to a bank
lessons.teaser: How debt was invented as a tool of domination long before capitalism.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Bullshit Jobs — why so much work under capitalism is meaningless by design
lessons.teaser: Why most work under capitalism is pointless by design.

lessons.title: Lesson 4: The Utopia of Rules — why bureaucracy is a problem for the left, not just the right
lessons.teaser: How paperwork, means-testing, and administration humiliate and exhaust people.

lessons.title: Lesson 5: The Dawn of Everything — what the deep past tells us about human freedom
lessons.teaser: What Occupy Wall Street and other movements actually built.

lessons.title: Lesson 6: Synthesis — what Graeber's work means for thinking and acting in Aotearoa today
lessons.teaser: Debt, work, bureaucracy, and direct democracy through a New Zealand lens.

courses.title: Pedagogy of the Algorithm — Freire mō Aotearoa
courses.description: AI Warrior Course 4. Paulo Freire's pedagogy of the oppressed, refitted for the age of generative AI. The chatbot is a banking-model classroom that talks back. Six lessons on dialogue, problem-posing, conscientização, and the kura kaupapa Māori parallel — Graham Hingangaroa Smith's kaupapa Māori praxis as Aotearoa's own refusal of the banking model. Sundays 19:00 NZST.

lessons.title: Course launch — Pedagogy of the Algorithm
lessons.teaser: Why Freire, why now. Every chatbot is a banking-model classroom. Freire's antidote, mapped onto kura kaupapa Māori praxis. Six lessons across six weeks.

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Ko wai a Freire? — the teacher who refused to deposit
lessons.teaser: Recife 1921 to São Paulo 1997. The man whose literacy method taught sugar-cane workers to read the word and the world — and got him 70 days in a military prison for it.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: The banking model goes industrial — every chatbot is a deposit
lessons.teaser: Freire's banking concept of education, retold for the age of generative AI. What happens when the teacher is the model and the deposit is your prompt history.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Problem-posing — how to force the chatbot to think with you
lessons.teaser: From banking to dialogue. Problem-posing as a praxis — the prompts, the discipline, the refusal to be a passive depositor of someone else's training data.

lessons.title: Lesson 4: Conscientização — critical consciousness in the algorithmic feed
lessons.teaser: Freire's central concept. Reading the word and the world when the world is a recommendation algorithm. How conscientização becomes a daily discipline, not a one-off awakening.

lessons.title: Lesson 5: Kura kaupapa Māori — Aotearoa's own refusal of the banking model
lessons.teaser: Graham Hingangaroa Smith's kaupapa Māori praxis read alongside Freire. Te reo revitalisation as a Freirean act. The pedagogy that built itself when the Crown's would not teach it.

lessons.title: Lesson 6: A Freirean praxis for the AI moment — what we teach next
lessons.teaser: Closing the loop. A working-class, kaupapa-aligned, AI-aware Freirean praxis. Generative themes for organisers, study circles, and the household. What we carry into Deleuze.

courses.title: Gilles Deleuze for Educators, Artists, and Organisers
courses.description: Rhizomes, assemblages, and lines of flight — Deleuze's ideas for those who teach, create, and organise.

lessons.title: Course intro — coming soon
lessons.teaser: Overview of the upcoming Deleuze course.

courses.title: Tangi o te Tāheke — Bakunin mō Aotearoa
courses.description: The final thinker in the AI Warrior series. Mikhail Bakunin's federalist, anti-authoritarian socialism — rendered for Aotearoa as a Zapatista-style kaupapa of autonomous communes, mana motuhake, and refusal of the state-capital machine. Six lessons. The waterfall tears down what dams the awa.

lessons.title: Course launch — Tangi o te Tāheke
lessons.teaser: The cry of the waterfall. Why Bakunin, why Aotearoa, why now — and how this course closes the AI Warrior thinkers series.

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Ko wai a Bakunin? — the man Marx feared
lessons.teaser: The Russian who split the First International, defended the Paris Commune, and warned that any state — even a workers' state — becomes a new prison.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Mana motuhake — federation from below, not the state from above
lessons.teaser: Bakunin's free federation read alongside He Whakaputanga and tino rangatiratanga. Power binds upward from the hapū, never downward from Wellington.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Te tāheke — the passion for destruction is also a creative passion
lessons.teaser: How to tear down without nihilism. The waterfall as kaupapa: what falls feeds the awa, what is demolished irrigates the kāinga.

lessons.title: Lesson 4: Caracoles in the kāinga — a Zapatista reading for Aotearoa
lessons.teaser: Chiapas to Ōtepoti. Autonomous councils, mandar obedeciendo, and what a Kiwi caracol looks like — marae, co-ops, learning circles, mutual-aid pātaka.

lessons.title: Lesson 5: Refusing the algorithmic Leviathan — anarchism in the age of AI
lessons.teaser: Bakunin called the state a machine. The machine is now literal. How federation, encryption, and refusal apply to platform capital and state AI.

lessons.title: Lesson 6: Arm the class — closing the AI Warrior thinkers series
lessons.teaser: From Gramsci to Bakunin in six thinkers. The toolkit, the refrain, the kaupapa for the next decade. E kore e mau te rongo ki te whenua kāore e watea.
